pi_items.source: prapare.org official PDF documentation and NACHC resources confirm the tool is referred to as 'PRAPARE Assessment Tool' or 'PRAPARE Assessment'

Multiple sources including prapare.org, NACHC (National Association of Community Health Centers), and peer-reviewed research confirm PRAPARE stands for 'Protocol for Responding to and Assessing Patients' Assets, Risks, and Experiences'

pi_items.metadata_value: Social determinants of health assessment concepts
pi_items.verified_value: Social determinants of health assessment concepts
pi_items.source: Official PRAPARE documentation, research articles (ScienceDirect, PubMed, NIH PMC), and NACHC resources all confirm that PRAPARE is specifically designed as a 'social determinants of health screening tool' and 'national standardized patient risk assessment tool designed to engage patients in assessing & addressing social drivers of health (SDOH)'

privileged_info_summary: All three metadata claims in the task are verified as accurate. PRAPARE Assessment is the official name of a real, evidence-based healthcare assessment tool owned by the National Association of Community Health Centers (NACHC). PRAPARE stands for 'Protocol for Responding to and Assessing Patients' Assets, Risks, and Experiences' and is specifically designed to assess social determinants of health (SDOH) in clinical settings. The tool is standardized across ICD-10, LOINC, and SNOMED, and is available in over 25 languages for use in healthcare systems and community health centers.
